sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 17.21 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.17)

statement.latex: a) If$A, B, C$are torsion-free abelian groups with$A A B C$, must$A A B$? b) What if, furthermore,$B C$? { G.\,M.\,Bergman} No, not necessarily, even in case b). See (A.\,L.\,S.\,Corner, in Proc. Colloq. Abelian Groups (Tihany, 1963), Budapest, 1964, 43--48); I also have an example with$B$and$C$of rank 1 as torsion-free abelian groups. (G.\,M.\,Bergman, Letter of 20 February 2011.)

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
